mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A labor market slack threshold breach above 4.5% unemployment signals deteriorating consumer spending capacity, which directly reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when UNRATE exceeds 4.5% for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which amplifies earnings revisions and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to generate a positive rank IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects immediate supply-side capacity utilization that supports short-term earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration coincides with negative 5-day forward returns in greater than 70% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation readings above 205.0 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL stays above 205.0 for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the aggregate wage bill and consumer purchasing power, providing a consistent positive driver for S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with positive forward returns, yielding a rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic demand strength, which validates corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive co-movement of GDPC1 and RSAFS is followed by negative 20-day returns in the majority of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side contraction that outweighs demand resilience, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 60-day returns in greater than 60% of cases.
